plan_history.intent_description: Traffic from 192.168.0.162 to 172.16.0.11 is blackholed by a forwarding loop: R1 and R3 have reciprocal static routes for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at each other; additionally the intended path via R2 is down (no OSPF neighbor). Desired outcome: break the loop, restore a correct route to 172.16.0.0/24 via R2, and re-establish R1–R2 adjacency so 192.168.0.162 can reach 172.16.0.11.
plan_history.problem: Reciprocal static routes on R1 and R3 create a forwarding loop/blackhole for 172.16.0.0/24; R2 path is currently unavailable (no OSPF neighbor on R1–R2).
plan_history.fix_summary: 1) Remove the two incorrect static routes that point R1 and R3 at each other. 2) Add a corrective static route on R1 pointing 172.16.0.0/24 toward R2 (assumed next-hop 10.0.0.2). 3) Ensure R1 Serial0/1/0 is administratively up to restore adjacency to R2.

plan_history.intent_description: R1 and R2 fail to form an OSPF adjacency because the 10.0.0.0/30 link is in area 0 on R1 and area 1 on R2, preventing R2 from learning/advertising 172.16.0.0/24 and causing traffic from 192.168.0.162 to 172.16.0.11 to be blackholed. Desired outcome: correct OSPF area mismatch, bring the R1–R2 adjacency up, and restore a correct route to 172.16.0.0/24 via R2.
plan_history.problem: OSPF area mismatch on the 10.0.0.0/30 link between router1 and router2 prevents adjacency; traffic to 172.16.0.0/24 is blackholed.
plan_history.fix_summary: Change router2's 10.0.0.0/30 interface (Serial0/1/0) to OSPF area 0 to match router1 and ensure the interface is administratively up. This will form the R1–R2 adjacency and allow R2 to learn/advertise 172.16.0.0/24. Risk: transient routing/traffic disruption during adjacency formation and SPF recalculation.
